name: NovArch
description: Photoactivatable archaerhodopsin-derived indicator whose near-infrared fluorescence is reversibly enhanced by one-photon blue or two-photon near-infrared excitation, enabling optically sectioned voltage imaging in tissue.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential voltage response under red-only illumination. Upstroke: fast tau=1.2 ms (76%) + slow tau=10 ms (24%) -> weighted mean 3.31 ms. Downstroke: fast tau=2.7 ms (76%) + slow tau=15 ms (24%) -> weighted mean 5.65 ms (Fig. 3F). Recording temperature not stated for the HEK293 voltage-clamp; assumed room temperature (25°C).

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF_R^V/F_R = 41 +/- 7% from -70 to +30 mV (100 mV step), n = 4 cells, HEK293 under wide-field red-only one-photon illumination (Fig. 3E)

sensitivityData.note: ΔF^V/F_R = 28 +/- 5% per action potential in cultured rat hippocampal neurons under wide-field red one-photon illumination (110 W/cm^2), n = 6 neurons (Fig. 4B).
ΔF^V.2P/F_R = 79 +/- 11% per action potential in cultured rat hippocampal neurons with 2P sensitization (900 nm scanned around cell periphery) added to red illumination, n = 6 neurons (Fig. 4B).

subthresholdData.note: Derived from 41% per 100 mV voltage sensitivity under red-only illumination (Fig. 3E)

brightnessData.note: Under red + blue light sensitization (the intended imaging modality), NovArch is ~3-fold brighter than Archon1 (Citrine-normalized: NovArch 0.88 ± 0.24 vs Archon1 0.30 ± 0.25). Under red-only illumination (no sensitization), NovArch is only ~0.7× Archon1 (Citrine ratio 0.2 ± 0.06). Raw counts: 1000 ± 510 (NovArch red+blue) vs 450 ± 440 (Archon1) at I_blue = 26 W/cm².

photostabilityData.note: NovArch photobleached to 50% of initial intensity in 650 s (10.8 min) under continuous high-intensity one-photon illumination (I_red = 140 W/cm^2 plus I_blue = 22 W/cm^2 photoactivation), Fig. S4C. Combined red+blue irradiance is not directly comparable to the single-color 100 mW/mm^2, 1 min normalization standard.

researchPapers.title: Photoactivated voltage imaging in tissue with an archaerhodopsin-derived reporter
researchPapers.authors: Chien MP, Brinks D, Testa-Silva G, Tian H, Brooks FP III, Adam Y, Bloxham W, Gmeiner B, Kheifets S, Cohen AE
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells, in vitro; cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro; mouse L5 cortical pyramidal neurons, acute brain slices; mouse hippocampus CA1, in vivo

researchPapers.title: Optical Switching Between Long-lived States of Opsin Transmembrane Voltage Sensors
researchPapers.authors: Mei G, Cavini CM, Mamaeva N, Wang P, DeGrip WJ, Rothschild KJ
researchPapers.sample: Purified NovArch protein reconstituted in membrane vesicles (FT-Raman and UV-vis spectroscopy of the photocycle); also QuasAr2
